text.title: Verb-Object Tool Naming with Namespace Prefix Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Tools named `search`, `manage`, `do_thing`. Eval shows 40% mis-selection across the catalog.
antipattern.root-cause: Single-token names collide with every other server's same verb; the model has no discrimination signal.

antipattern.symptom: MCP gateway exposes 12 servers; tools `search`, `list`, `create` collide across all of them.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought MCP gateway would namespace automatically; it does not.
antipattern.fix: Mandatory prefix at registration time: `__`; gateway fails build on collision.

antipattern.symptom: Tool name is `acme_corp_internal_v2_search_documents_advanced`. Prompt budget for tool list exceeds 4K tokens.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought longer names = more discoverable. Long names consume context and hurt the lexical match.
antipattern.fix: Cap names at 6 tokens; drop marketing nouns; rely on description for nuance.

antipattern.symptom: Catalog renamed in bulk to follow `verb_object` but no pre/post eval. Tool-selection accuracy drops.
antipattern.root-cause: Renames done without eval baseline → cannot tell which renames helped vs hurt.
antipattern.fix: Per-rename A/B on the eval set; revert renames that regress.
